statement.markdown: You are given a staircase problem where you have to climb `n` stairs, and at each step, you can either move one step or two steps forward. The goal is to find the number of unique ways to climb the stairs. However, the given code has a logical error.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an integer `n` representing the number of stairs.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the total number of unique ways to climb the stairs.
statement.notes: The solution should be efficient and handle large inputs.

examples.explanation: There are 3 unique ways to climb 3 stairs: 1+1+1, 1+2, 2+1

hints.text: Carefully analyze the recurrence relation and find the error in the given code.

editorial.title: Incorrect Staircase Climbing Editorial
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves a bottom-up dynamic programming solution with two variables to store the number of ways to climb i-1 and i-2 stairs.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive recursive solution with exponential time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: Use two variables to store the previous two values in the recurrence relation.
editorial.optimalSolution: Dynamic programming solution with two variables and a bottom-up approach.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: By induction, we can show that the dynamic programming solution produces the correct result.
commonMistakes: Using a single variable to store the recurrence relation

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int climbStairs(int n) { return climbStairs(n, 0); } private: int climbStairs(int n, int i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return climbStairs(n, i + 1) + climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int climbStairs(int n) { return climbStairs(n, 0); } private int climbStairs(int n, int i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return climbStairs(n, i + 1) + climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def climbStairs(self, n): return self.climbStairs(n, 0) def climbStairs(self, n, i): if i == n: return 1 if i > n: return 0 return self.climbStairs(n, i + 1) + self.climbStairs(n, i + 2)
starterCode.javascript: class Solution { climbStairs(n) { return this.climbStairs(n, 0); } climbStairs(n, i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return this.climbStairs(n, i + 1) + this.climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int climbStairs(int n) { return climbStairs(n, 0); } private: int climbStairs(int n, int i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return climbStairs(n, i + 2) + climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int climbStairs(int n) { return climbStairs(n, 0); } private int climbStairs(int n, int i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return climbStairs(n, i + 2) + climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def climbStairs(self, n): return self.climbStairs(n, 0) def climbStairs(self, n, i): if i == n: return 1 if i > n: return 0 return self.climbStairs(n, i + 2) + self.climbStairs(n, i + 2)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class Solution { climbStairs(n) { return this.climbStairs(n, 0); } climbStairs(n, i) { if (i == n) { return 1; } if (i > n) { return 0; } return this.climbStairs(n, i + 2) + this.climbStairs(n, i + 2); } }

solutions.cpp: class Solution {public: int climbStairs(int n) { int dp[n + 1]; dp[1] = 1; dp[2] = 2; for (int i = 3; i <= n; ++i) { dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2]; } return dp[n]; } };
solutions.java: public class Solution { public int climbStairs(int n) { int[] dp = new int[n + 1]; dp[1] = 1; dp[2] = 2; for (int i = 3; i <= n; ++i) { dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2]; } return dp[n]; } }
solutions.python: class Solution: def climbStairs(self, n: int) -> int: dp = [0] * (n + 1); dp[1] = 1; dp[2] = 2; for i in range(3, n + 1): dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2]; return dp[n];
solutions.javascript: class Solution { climbStairs(n) { let dp = new Array(n + 1); dp[1] = 1; dp[2] = 2; for (let i = 3; i <= n; ++i) { dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2]; } return dp[n]; } }
solutions.c++: #include <iostream> using namespace std; int climbStairs(int n) { int dp[n + 1]; dp[1] = 1; dp[2] = 2; for (int i = 3; i <= n; ++i) { dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2]; } return dp[n]; } int main() { int n; cin >> n; cout << climbStairs(n) << endl; }
